titleStmt.title: Telegraph from Frongoch to Sir Robert Chalmers, 2 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Telegraph to Sir Robert Chalmers regarding release of 35 Irish prisoners.
In 1916 Col. F.A. Heygate Lambert (1857-1929) was serving as the Commandant of Frongoch Internment Camp. Lambert served in the camp until the last of the prisoners were released in December 1916. In the Spring of 1917 he went to Ypres to command a battalion of the Labour Corps.
Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was appointed under-secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the aftermath of the Easter Rising.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Inspector General To see return Chief 08-02-16 Message Received in Chief Secretary's Office Under Secretary Dublin Castle
Dublin The Under Secretary
noted + Returned
Comm 3/8
C362 following 11 Irish prisoners
will be released at 8
pm tonight in addition to
list already sent you aaa
They will travel by Holyhead
— Kingstown route by the
midnight service aaa list of
35 to be released tomorrow
by express post tonight 1043
sargent P. a. 59 Hollybank Rd Drumcondra 884 Garrett
(2) J. 2 Shannon Street Enniscorthy
1166 Howley was Limepath
1783 Millen Patk 9 Emerald
Lee Cork st Dublin 1096
Higgins w. Cashla athenry galway
1306 Costello mc glenscaul oranmore
Co galway 54 Connell John
Clarenbridge Co galway 1397 = B.
Rongham B. Derrough Colemanstown 1471 =
B. quinn g. 61 Cork st
Dublin 411 Cooper John 33
(3) Lennox St Dublin 1445 = a
Waters John 8 (or 6)
East James St Dublin. Prison warden Frongach
Miscellaneous cases. Exemptions Galway 28/7/16 1306 Higgins, Patrick Lisheenkyle; Athenry Dublin City. (S.O.) 26/7/1916 582 BERMINGHAM, John James. St. Ignatius Rd.
26/7/1916 1162 C BILLINGS, Joseph Patrick.
26/7/1916 1155 CANNEY, Daniel. North Strand road
do 1528 C GREGORY, John Fairview.
28/7/1916 525 A HEGARTY, John. Kingstown. Cork. (S.O.) 28/7/1916 917 HEGARTY, Daniel .J. Mallow

do 1279 McGuirk, Patrick Dungannon.
